user.title: Battery Charge Calculator – Connection & Inverter
user.description: Enter your GivEnergy and Octopus Energy API credentials, then configure your inverter and battery.

data.simulate_only: Simulate only (do not send charge commands)

data_description.inverter_size_kw: Maximum continuous power output of the inverter in kilowatts (e.g. 3.6).
data_description.inverter_efficiency: Round-trip efficiency as a decimal. 0.9 means 10% losses.
data_description.battery_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of the battery pack in kilowatt-hours.
data_description.base_load_kwh_30min: Average non-heating electricity consumption per 30-minute slot in kWh (e.g. 0.25 = 500 W continuous). Leave at default if unsure.

heating.title: Heating Configuration – Type
heating.description: Choose how heating demand is calculated.

data_description.heating_type: 'none' = no electric heating; 'interpolation' = custom (temp, kWh) curve; 'electric' = heat loss model (direct electric or heat pump).

heating_interpolation.title: Heating Configuration – Custom Curve
heating_interpolation.description: Provide measured (outdoor temperature, energy use) pairs. The calculator will fit a curve through them.

data.heating_known_points: Known (temperature °C, energy kWh per 30-min slot) pairs – JSON array

data_description.heating_known_points: JSON array of [temperature_°C, kWh_per_30min] pairs, e.g. [[-6, 0.75], [0, 0.55], [6, 0.30], [15, 0.0]]. Energy values are kWh consumed in a single 30-minute slot.

heating_electric.title: Heating Configuration – Heat Pump / Direct Electric
heating_electric.description: Configure the heating system efficiency. Heat loss is entered on the next screen.

data_description.heating_cop: COP at the A7 standard test condition (outdoor 7 °C). Use 1.0 for direct electric resistance heating. Typical air-source heat pump COP: 2.5–4.0.
data_description.heating_indoor_temp: The temperature you want to maintain inside the building in degrees Celsius (e.g. 21).
data_description.heating_flow_temp: Temperature of water delivered to radiators or underfloor heating in °C. Typical values: 45–55 °C for radiators, 35–40 °C for underfloor. Used to calculate how COP varies with outdoor temperature.

heat_loss_method.title: Heating Configuration – Heat Loss Method
heat_loss_method.description: Choose how to supply the building's heat loss coefficient.

data_description.heat_loss_method: 'known' = enter the W/°C value directly; 'report' = enter the total Watts from a heat loss report plus the design temperatures used; 'estimate' = the calculator will estimate it from your building's properties.

heat_loss_report.title: Heating Configuration – Heat Loss Report Values
heat_loss_report.description: Enter the figures directly from your heat loss survey report. The W/°C coefficient is derived automatically.

data.heat_loss_report_watts: Total building heat loss from report (W)
data.heat_loss_report_outdoor_temp: Outdoor design temperature used in report (°C)
data.heat_loss_report_indoor_temp: Average indoor design temperature used in report (°C)

data_description.heat_loss_report_watts: The 'Total (kW)' figure from the report summary, converted to Watts (multiply kW × 1000). E.g. 8.3 kW → 8300 W.
data_description.heat_loss_report_outdoor_temp: The external design temperature stated in the report assumptions (e.g. -3.2 °C). This is the coldest outdoor temperature the system was sized for.
data_description.heat_loss_report_indoor_temp: The average indoor target temperature across all rooms (e.g. 20 °C). Use a weighted average if rooms differ significantly.

heat_loss_known.title: Heating Configuration – Heat Loss Value
heat_loss_known.description: Enter the total fabric and ventilation heat loss of the building.

data_description.heating_heat_loss: Watts of heat lost per degree Celsius of temperature difference between inside and outside (W/°C). A typical semi-detached house is 100–200 W/°C. You can find this on a heat loss survey report.

building_estimate.title: Heating Configuration – Building Properties
building_estimate.description: The calculator will estimate your building's heat loss from these properties.

data_description.building_floor_area: Sum of all heated floor areas in square metres (e.g. 85 for a typical 3-bed semi).
data_description.building_age: Approximate period the building was constructed. Older buildings have higher heat loss.
data_description.building_wall_type: Primary external wall construction. Affects the U-value used in the estimate.
data_description.building_glazing: Type of glazing on the majority of windows.

ml_settings.title: ML Power Estimation
ml_settings.description: Optional: enable the machine-learning power model. The ML service runs as a separate Docker container and handles training and inference. Enter the service URL and API key below.

data_description.ml_service_url: Full HTTPS URL of your BCC ML Service instance, e.g. https://192.168.1.50:8765
data_description.ml_service_api_key: API key printed to the service container log on first start.
data_description.ml_service_tls_fingerprint: SHA-256 fingerprint of the service TLS certificate (colon-delimited hex), printed on first start. Leave blank to use OS CA bundle.
data_description.ml_consumption_source: 'GivEnergy' uses the inverter cloud API; 'Octopus' uses your smart meter half-hourly reads via the Octopus Energy API; 'Both' fetches from both and averages.
data_description.octopus_mpn: 13-digit MPAN shown on your electricity bill or Octopus account page (e.g. 1012345678900). Required when source is Octopus or Both.
data_description.octopus_meter_serial: Serial number printed on your electricity meter. Required when source is Octopus or Both.
data_description.ml_training_lookback_days: How many days of history to use for training. More data generally improves accuracy. Minimum 14, maximum 730 (2 years).

tariff_comparison.title: Monthly Tariff Comparison
tariff_comparison.description: Enable this to compare your electricity costs across different Octopus tariffs using the last month of real meter data.

data_description.tariff_comparison_enabled: When enabled, a new sensor will be created showing monthly cost breakdowns for each selected tariff.

tariff_comparison_pick.title: Select Tariffs to Compare
tariff_comparison_pick.description: Choose the Octopus import tariffs you want to compare. Your current tariff ({region} region) is pre-selected. Results appear in the Monthly Tariff Comparison sensor.

data_description.tariff_comparison_tariffs: Select one or more tariffs. Your current tariff should be included so you can see what you actually paid vs. what you would have paid.

export_meter.title: Export Meter & Solar (optional)
export_meter.description: Enter your export MPAN and meter serial to include export earnings in tariff comparisons. Optionally enter your solar production entity to use historical generation data in simulations.

data_description.octopus_export_mpn: The 13-digit MPAN for your export meter. Found on your Octopus account or electricity bill.
data_description.octopus_export_meter_serial: The serial number printed on your export electricity meter.
data_description.solar_energy_entity: Entity ID of your solar cumulative energy sensor (e.g. sensor.solar_energy_production). Must be a total_increasing kWh sensor that appears in the HA Energy dashboard. Leave blank to use zero solar in simulations.

error.invalid_design_temps: Indoor temperature must be higher than outdoor design temperature.
error.tariff_fetch_failed: Could not fetch available tariffs from Octopus — check your API key and internet connection.
error.select_at_least_one: Please select at least one tariff.

options.report: I have a heat loss survey report
